title: Eriolobus tschonoskii
intro: Eriolobus tschonoskii, also known as the Chinese spiny buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of southwestern China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Eriolobus tschonoskii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Eriolobus tschonoskii is often prepared as a decoction, pill, or extract, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including joint pain, gastrointestinal disorders, and respiratory infections.

It is classified in the genus Eriolobus, which contains only this species. Eriolobus tschonoskii is native to China and is often found in mountainous regions.
